Maloo Test Queue: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_queue?jobs=lustre-reviews&builds=101128&commit=Apply+Filter
Maloo Results: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/related?jobs=lustre-reviews&builds=101128#redirect

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

For future reference, using the "always_except" helper is the preferred way to do this, but the next patch reverts the change and I'd prefer to get this landed as it is pretty disruptive to testing.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Chris, the recent landing of patch https://review.whamcloud.com/49361 "LU-10391 lnet: update ping to handle multiple NIDs" is causing sanity-lnet test_253/254 to fail about 90% of the time.

Those tests were addeed in your patch https://review.whamcloud.com/49078 "LU-16303 lnet: Drop LNet message if deadline exceeded", but I can't make any assessment of how serious the regression is.  Is it OK to exclude those tests (i.e. is it just a test script timing issue) or is there more broken with ping that might need the patch to be reverted?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> Chris, the recent landing of patch https://review.whamcloud.com/49361 "LU-10391 lnet: update ping to handle multiple NIDs" is causing sanity-lnet test_253/254 to fail about 90% of the time.
> 
> Those tests were addeed in your patch https://review.whamcloud.com/49078 "LU-16303 lnet: Drop LNet message if deadline exceeded", but I can't make any assessment of how serious the regression is.  Is it OK to exclude those tests (i.e. is it just a test script timing issue) or is there more broken with ping that might need the patch to be reverted?

Yes, I think it is fine to exclude them. The patch probably modified the behavior of ping such that it affected the timing of the test. I don't think it actually broke the LU-16303 behavior.

commit.subject: LU-10391 tests: except sanity-lnet/253+254 due to failures
commit.message:
LU-10391 tests: except sanity-lnet/253+254 due to failures

Moving to Netlink for ping support has exposed a timing issues
with some of the sanity-lnet test.  Skip those tests temporarily
until the issue can be fixed.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Change-Id: Ia30fcbf8eca7baaf6f896e6e51229c69cea82804
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53593
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
